 {"id":"gt-ihzqr","title":"Formula SDK: Developer Tooling for Workflow Authors","description":"SDK for creating, testing, debugging, and validating formulas.

## Components

### Forge (Creation)
```bash
mol forge new my-workflow        # Scaffold new formula
mol forge extend base-workflow   # Create variant
mol forge lint my-workflow       # Check syntax/style
```

### Lab (Testing)
```bash
mol lab run my-workflow --dry-run   # Mock execution
mol lab test my-workflow            # Run formula tests
mol lab trace my-workflow           # Step-through debug
```

### Validator (Verification)
```bash
mol validate my-workflow                    # Check well-formedness
mol validate my-workflow --protocol=Reviewable  # Check protocol compliance
mol validate my-workflow --schematic=shiny  # Check schematic compatibility
```

### Analyzer (Static Analysis)
```bash
mol analyze my-workflow --deps     # Dependency graph
mol analyze my-workflow --coverage # Step coverage
mol analyze my-workflow --cycles   # Detect cycles
```

## Relationship to Other Concepts

The SDK operates on:
- Formulas (source level)
- Protos (compiled level)
- Molecules (runtime level)

And validates against:
- Protocols (type system)
- Schematics (domain composition)

## Open Questions

1. Bundled vs separate - mol subcommands or formula-kit binary?
2. IDE integration - LSP for formula files?
3. Test harness - how to mock steps? fixtures?

## Related

- docs/formula_evolution.md - "SDK Question" section
- gt-8tmz.30 - Proto debugging tools (subset)
- gt-8tmz.31 - Formula validation (subset)

 {"id":"gt-il2p7","title":"bd update --claim for work queue semantics","description":"Atomic claim operation for work queue messages.

## Deliverables

1. Add --claim flag to bd update
2. Atomic operation:
   - Sets assignee to claimer
   - Sets status to in_progress
   - Fails if already claimed
3. Conflict detection:
   - If already claimed, return error: "already claimed by X"
4. Integration with gt mail for queue messages

## Example
```bash
gt mail queue cleanup/gastown  # See available work
bd update msg-xxx --claim       # Claim it atomically
# Returns error if someone else claimed first
```

## Dependencies
- queue: shared storage (gt-???)

## Acceptance
- --claim sets assignee + status atomically
- Double-claim returns clear error
- Works with queue messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:08.186488-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:08.186488-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-il2p7","depends_on_id":"gt-tnap3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:53:08.553425-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-kc7yj.2","title":"Witness swarm integration","description":"Add swarm coordination to Witness patrol.

The Witness should be able to:
1. Receive swarm assignments via mail (SWARM: subject prefix)
2. Query ready front and dispatch to available polecats
3. Monitor polecat progress on swarm tasks
4. Detect completion and close swarm molecule
5. Use Christmas Ornament pattern for arm-bonding (optional)

Implementation:
- Add swarm handling to mol-witness-patrol formula
- Mail trigger: SWARM in subject
- Dispatch: bd ready --parent=<epic> to find ready issues
- Assign: gt sling <issue> to available polecat
- Monitor: Check polecat status during patrol
- Complete: When bd ready --parent returns empty and all closed

Reference: ~/gt/docs/swarm-architecture.md - Witness Integration section

Dependencies: gt-kc7yj.1 (gt swarm refactor)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:12:41.909263-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-29T14:21:32.739182-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T14:21:32.739182-08:00","close_reason":"Added dispatch-swarm-work step to mol-witness-patrol formula. Witness can now dispatch ready swarm tasks to idle polecats via gt sling.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kc7yj.2","depends_on_id":"gt-kc7yj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:12:41.909767-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-kc7yj.2","depends_on_id":"gt-kc7yj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:12:53.340127-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-kc7yj.3","title":"Swarm completion detection and auto-close","description":"Detect when all epic children are closed and auto-close the swarm molecule.

Core logic:
1. After any issue closes, check if it was part of a swarm
2. Query bd list --parent=<epic> --status=open
3. If empty (all closed), close the swarm molecule
4. Fire completion event for activity feed

Can be implemented as:
- Part of Witness patrol
- Or as a bd hook triggered on issue close
- Or as a gt swarm check command called periodically

Reference: ~/gt/docs/swarm-architecture.md - Recovery Protocol

 {"id":"gt-kc7yj.4","title":"End-to-end swarm integration test","description":"Full integration test for swarm lifecycle.

Test scenario:
1. Create a small epic with 3-4 issues and dependencies
2. Run bd swarm validate - should pass
3. Run gt swarm create with the epic
4. Verify swarm molecule created with mol_type=swarm
5. Run gt swarm status - verify ready front
6. Simulate issue completion (bd close)
7. Verify ready front advances
8. Complete all issues
9. Verify swarm auto-closes

Can be manual test documented in test file, or automated.

 {"id":"gt-kfznm","title":"Refinery: Send MERGED notification to witness after merge","description":"During liftoff test (gt-j0gx2), observed that refinery:
1. Received MERGE_READY for nux
2. Fetched, rebased, resolved conflicts correctly
3. Ran tests (passed)
4. Merged to main and pushed
5. Deleted polecat branch

But did NOT:
- Send MERGED mail to witness
- Close the MR bead (gt-5qkah)

The mol-refinery-patrol.formula.toml documents this step but the agent didn't execute it.

Impact:
- Witness never receives MERGED signal
- Polecat worktree never gets nuked
- MR beads accumulate as open

Fix:
After successful merge+push, refinery must:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "MERGED <polecat>" -m "..."
bd close <mr-bead> --reason "Merged to main"
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:26:08.423116-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-28T16:30:17.040435-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T16:30:17.040435-08:00"}
